#FDFEFF (or 0xFDFEFF) unknown color: approx Alice Blue. HEX triplet: FD, and FF. RGB value (253,254,255). Sum RGB (Red+Green+Blue) 253+254+255=762 (100% max value 765). Red value 253 (99.22% from 255 33.20% from 762); Green value 254 (99.61% from 255 33.33% from 762); Blue value 255 (100% from 255 33.46% from 762); Max value from RGB 255 color contains mainly: blue. Hex color #FDFEFF not web safe color. Web safe color analog (approx): #FFFFFF. Inversed color #FDFEFF #020100. Grayscale: #FDFDFD. Windows color (decimal): -131329 16776957. OLE color: 16776957.
HSL color representation color #FDFEFF: hue angle 210o degrees, saturation: lightness: 1%. HSV value (or HSB Brightness) color and HSV saturation: 0.01%. Process color model (Four color, CMYK) #FDFEFF Cyan 0.01, Magento 0.00, Yellow and Black CMYK)

Okay, you'll all take your seats and quiet down can begin. I'll keep this very short today because know it's Spring and many you are eager get out there and walk Frisbees and throw dogs... mean well you know what mean.

"standing still" and "moving past" then sees moving relative himself. But how can sure actually the still one while the moving one?
right next say we're sitting bench can obviously tell he's not moving closer further away from the bench. can prove this reaching out and putting hand his shoulder any time reassure ourselves hasn't moved.
see pass by, observe her distance decrease she gets closer and then increase she gets further. she passes closely enough, might able "high-five" she passes. So, clearly she's moving.

minor caveat that the sound and vibration the train running along the tracks provides clue that we're moving.
But you watched the old Mission: Impossible show, you may have seen that episode where they trick their target into believing he's moving train using sound and mechanical effects.
For that matter, some amusement park rides pretty good job tricking you into thinking that you're moving using the same kinds effects.
Conversely, the train car sound proof and has smooth suspension, you may feel sense motion. Consider what feels like ride commercial aircraft. You can walk down the aisle with sense that you're really hurtling through the air hundreds miles per hour!
important term here frame reference. It's technical term for "point view" but includes the idea that for example you, Al, the bench you're sitting on, and the entire train car, are part one thing: frame reference.

this situation, both observers can make equally valid claims they are standing still while the other moving.

CMYK color space, consist 32.9% cyan, magenta, 49.7% yellow and 41.6% black, whereas that corresponding CMYK values are 0.33, 0.5, 0.42.
